title: Cannot Run Podman in Basic Chroot
body:
### Podman Breaks in simple Chroot

When setting up a simple chroot, podman fails if invoked within the chroot with a cryptic error. 

The chroot is setup with:
```
chroot_dir="$1"
sub_dirs=("bin" "lib" "lib64" "proc" "sys" "usr" "etc" "dev" "var" "run")

for dir in "${sub_dirs[@]}"; do
    bind_point="${chroot_dir}/${dir}"
    mkdir -p "${bind_point}"
    echo ${bind_point}
    bindfs "/${dir}" "${bind_point}"
done
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a basic chroot
2. Try to run `podman --version` from within the chroot:
```
sudo chroot test_chroot_environment podman --version
```

### Describe the results you received

```
cannot retrieve cmd line%
```
The "cannot retrieve cmd line" error above is cryptic, and I'm not sure what part of the chroot is breaking podman. 

### Describe the results you expected

Able to print podman version within chroot, then run a basic container

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Chroot (see above)

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: Podman expects there to be a properly setup /proc and /sys.  Also would want to communicate with systemd.

AFAIK, chroot won't allow you to run as rootless, but should be fine with root.

Why are you using bindfs?  Does it work any differently if you use `mount --bind`.
@LSaldyt have you tried with a regular bind mount?
